headline: Immediate governance overhaul needed to address ₹163.0 Cr SEBI penalty and restore investor confidence
board_paragraph: YES Bank faces a material governance challenge following a ₹96.5 Cr SEBI penalty (engine estimate) for bond mis-selling, with total exposure at ₹163.0 Cr (REQUIRED). The board must approve a comprehensive governance remediation plan within 8 weeks to avoid further regulatory escalation and reputational damage. Failure to act risks additional sanctions, credit rating downgrades, and up to ₹66.5 Cr (engine estimate) in indirect costs. Delay could trigger investor exits and higher cost of capital, directly impacting FY27-29 performance.

stakeholder_map.stance: Enforcement escalation if remediation plan absent within 30-60 days. Formal show-cause → interim penalty → appeal window. Settlement via Consent Mechanism in 40-60% of cases.
Direct intervention via moratorium, AQR, or governance restructuring for material asset-quality or fraud events. Climate stress test framework applies to top-16 banks from FY27.

AGAINST vote recommendation on audit committee chair or CEO re-election for material governance failures. Downgrade of governance rating (MSCI/ISS QualityScore) by 1-2 bands. AGM season cadence: April-September.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Infosys 2017 Sikka exit → ISS flagged 2 AGMs

stakeholder_map.stance: Rating action (e.g., BBB → BB) within 90-180 days of material event. Watch-list status in interim. Flow-through to MSCI ESG Leaders / SRI indices triggers 1 (engine estimate)-3% AUM outflow from passive strategies.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Vedanta 2020 B→CCC → MSCI ESG Leaders exclusion

stakeholder_map.stakeholder: BlackRock + Norges Bank IM + CalPERS (Top-10 AUM)
stakeholder_map.stance: Private engagement first (letter/meeting) within 30-60 days. Vote against if remediation absent by next AGM. Exclusion from ESG mandate AUM if severity=high sustained 2+ quarters. Position review on 8-12% AUM flows.
stakeholder_map.precedent: NBIM 2017 excluded 11 coal miners incl. Adani Ports

three_year_trajectory.do_nothing: If no remediation is undertaken, by FY27-29 YES Bank risks cumulative losses of ₹163.0 Cr (engine estimate) (REQUIRED) from penalties and reputational fallout. Credit ratings may be downgraded, triggering 1-3% AUM outflows and up to 40 bps increase in cost of capital. Investor confidence will erode, with proxy advisors likely to recommend against key board reappointments and ESG index exclusion probable.
three_year_trajectory.act_now: Implementing a robust governance remediation plan at a cost of ₹163.0 Cr (REQUIRED) can stabilize ratings, restore ESG index eligibility, and reduce cost of capital by up to 40 bps (engine estimate). Investor and regulator confidence is likely to recover within 12-18 months, supporting sustainable growth through FY27-29.

qna_drafts.earnings_call: We acknowledge the SEBI penalty of ₹96.5 Cr (engine estimate) and are taking decisive steps to strengthen our governance framework. Our board has approved a comprehensive remediation plan, and we are engaging proactively with all stakeholders to restore trust and ensure long-term value creation.
qna_drafts.press_statement: YES Bank recognizes the governance lapses identified by SEBI and has committed ₹163.0 Cr (engine estimate) (REQUIRED) to a full remediation program. We are implementing enhanced controls and transparency measures to address all concerns and reaffirm our commitment to best-in-class governance standards.
qna_drafts.board_qa: Q: max penalty? A: The total exposure is ₹163.0 Cr (engine estimate) (REQUIRED), including the SEBI penalty and indirect costs. Q: FY26 capex impact? A: The remediation plan will be funded within existing capex envelopes, with no material impact on core growth projects. Q: What if we delay? A: Delay risks further regulatory action, credit downgrades, and investor exits, compounding financial and reputational losses.
qna_drafts.regulator_qa: We appreciate SEBI’s guidance and are committed to full compliance. Our board has approved a time-bound remediation plan, with transparent progress reporting. We will submit detailed updates within the prescribed 8-week window and remain available for ongoing engagement to ensure all regulatory expectations are met.

warnings: verifier: added 6 source tags on ₹ figures
